pattern: Walk three consecutive monotone runs (up, down, up) from the front, requiring each to advance at least one step and the third to finish exactly at the end.
hints: The array must split into three back-to-back stretches: rising, then falling, then rising again, with the turning points strictly inside.
Greedily consume the first strictly increasing run, then a strictly decreasing run, then a strictly increasing run.

Start at index 0, climb while values increase (the peak must be past index 0), descend while they decrease (the valley must be before the last index), then climb again and check you land exactly on the final index.

edge_inputs.description: too short to form three runs
peak at index 0 is not allowed
valley at last index is not allowed
has a trailing flat run after final climb

stress.description: random small arrays over a narrow range so valid and invalid shapes both appear
stress.note: narrow range produces frequent equal-neighbour traps
